summary: Unauthenticated REST provider endpoint allows injection of arbitrary routing configuration via PUT request to /api/providers/rest, enabling complete traffic takeover through malicious catch-all route
expected_security_impact: Complete routing infrastructure compromise - attacker can redirect all HTTP traffic to attacker-controlled backends, intercept sensitive data, disrupt services, and perform lateral movement within infrastructure
actual_observed_result: Malicious configuration successfully injected via unauthenticated PUT request. High-priority catch-all route (PathPrefix:/) created and activated, routing all traffic to attacker-specified backend (http://attacker.example.com:9999). Configuration immediately applied by Traefik without validation.
verification_method: Automated verification via verify.sh checks API endpoints (/api/providers/rest and /api/frontends) to confirm malicious frontend and backend appear in active configuration
evidence_produced_by_run: PUT request to /api/providers/rest accepted without authentication
Backend 'malicious-backend' configured pointing to attacker URL
Configuration visible in API response confirming successful injection

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
